title: Microlicia sampaioana
taxonomy: Microlicia sampaioana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the melastome family or the tropical sorrel family. The plant is native to Brazil and belongs to the genus Microlicia, which comprises a group of small shrubs or subshrubs. Microlicia sampaioana is further classified within the tribe Microlicieae, which is a subfamily within the Melastomataceae family. The classification of Microlicia sampaioana is based on its unique characteristics, including its small size, simple leaves, and small, white flowers. By understanding its classification, scientists can better study and appreciate the unique features of this plant species.
uses: Microlicia sampaioana is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Although limited information is available, it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's extracts have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antiparasitic activities, which could be beneficial in the treatment of various infections. Its leaves and stems may be used as a natural remedy in traditional medicine, but more studies are required to confirm its efficacy.

intro: Microlicia sampaioana is a plant species that belongs to the Melastomataceae family, commonly known as the melastome family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Amazon region, for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, contributing to the treatment of various ailments such as fever, pain, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Microlicia sampaioana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, colds, and skin irritations.
